title: Wright's Barbecue Pulls Permit for Second Little Rock Location Near Chenal

seo.title: Wright's Barbecue Coming to West Little Rock Near Chenal | Metro Little Rock News
seo.description: Wright's Barbecue filed a permit for 800 Rahling Road near Chenal, planning a 5,000-square-foot building and exterior smokehouse for a fall 2026 opening.

summary: Wright's Barbecue has filed a building permit for a second Little Rock location at 800 Rahling Road near the Promenade at Chenal, with a roughly 5,000-square-foot standalone building and exterior smokehouse. The owner is targeting an October or November 2026 opening.

body_sections.body: Wright's Barbecue — the Arkansas concept that grew from a single Johnson location into a multi-city regional chain — has filed a building permit for 800 Rahling Road in West Little Rock, near the Promenade at Chenal. The declared construction value is $4.5 million. Plans call for a 5,000-square-foot building with an exterior smokehouse and covered outdoor seating. The Rebsamen Park Road location has been Wright's only standalone outpost within the city of Little Rock.

body_sections.heading: Timeline
body_sections.body: Wright's founder and CEO has said the West Little Rock spot is "probably about four months out, maybe five" from opening — with October or November 2026 given as the realistic target. A formal construction timeline hasn't been released, but the permit filing puts the project on track for a fall opening barring significant delays.

body_sections.heading: Chenal Context
body_sections.body: The Rahling Road corridor near the Promenade is dense with chain restaurants serving the surrounding residential subdivisions. Wright's represents a different category: a regional concept with a reputation built on wood-smoked brisket and ribs produced at a pace that prioritizes quality over throughput. The nearest comparable barbecue in the area has been the national chains, which Wright's consistently outperforms.

impact_note: A Chenal-area Wright's makes the brand accessible to the westside population without a trip to Rebsamen — which is a meaningful time savings during lunch. The open question is whether the build-out quality and smokehouse output hold up at a second location. That's the variable worth watching as they scale.
